cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out the best title for this passage. The passage starts by talking about the London Olympics being a success in terms of being the "greenest" Games ever. It mentions using recycled materials, cleaning up polluted areas, green building techniques, waste management, and encouraging public transport to reduce carbon emissions. It also briefly mentions the anti-doping efforts as part of the greenness, but that seems like a smaller part of the overall focus. Looking at the options: (A) How to deal with the anti-doping – This is mentioned at the end, but it's not the main focus. The passage is more about environmental initiatives than doping. (B) How to use recycled materials – While this is a part of the passage (recycled materials in construction), the passage covers more than just that. It also talks about green building techniques, water conservation, public transport, etc. (C) London's green games – This seems like a good fit. The entire passage discusses how London made the Olympics environmentally friendly,
